author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
Grace, Concealed in the Old Testament, is Revealed in the New.
title: Grace, Concealed in the Old Testament, is Revealed in the New.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Chapter 27 [XV.]—Grace, Concealed in the Old Testament, is Revealed in the New.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: This grace hid itself under a veil in the Old Testament, but it has been revealed in the New Testament according to the most perfectly ordered dispensation of the ages, forasmuch as God knew how to dispose all things.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And perhaps it is a part of this hiding of grace, that in the Decalogue, which was given on Mount Sinai, only the portion which relates to the Sabbath was hidden under a prefiguring precept.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: The Sabbath is a day of sanctification; and it is not without significance that, among all the works which God accomplished, the first sound of sanctification was heard on the day when He rested from all His labours.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: On this, indeed, we must not now enlarge.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: But at the same time I deem it to be enough for the point now in question, that it was not for nothing that the nation was commanded on that day to abstain from all servile work, by which sin is signified; but because not to commit sin belongs to sanctification, that is, to God’s gift through the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And this precept alone among the others, was placed in the law, which was written on the two tables of stone, in a prefiguring shadow, under which the Jews observe the Sabbath, that by this very circumstance it might be signified that it was then the time for concealing the grace, which had to be revealed in the New Testament by the death of Christ,—the rending, as it were, of the veil.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “For when,” says the apostle, “it shall turn to the Lord, the veil shall be taken away.”
